title: Encrypt secrets with ansible-vault
description: Keep credentials in vault-encrypted files, never in plaintext vars or playbooks.
body: Use when a playbook needs passwords, keys, or tokens. Do not place secrets in plaintext group_vars or inline in tasks — they then leak into version control and logs. Actions: store sensitive values in ansible-vault-encrypted files, reference them as variables, and mark tasks that handle them with no_log to keep values out of output. Validation signals include the repository containing only encrypted vault payloads and no plaintext credentials. Risk boundary: a committed plaintext secret must be treated as compromised — rotate it and escalate rather than just removing the line.
